governing_constraint: Harden the OAuth/identity surface for safe exposure. Federate any JWKS IdM (Ory reference), never an IdP. Single-binary, jsonwebtoken@9 pin. Fail-closed + degrades_to_deny on every new auth path; audit every authorize entry point.

choice: axum tower layer on the OAuth sub-router
decisions.rationale: RFC 7662 §2.1 MANDATES introspection-endpoint auth to stop token scanning; client-credentials is the standard mechanism and flint-gate already verifies them (client-credentials grant). The in-process governor layer already exists and is composable per-endpoint. Zero new crates.
decisions.fallback: network-restriction + rate-limit only if client-auth wiring slips
open_questions: /oauth/token hard client-auth gate vs rate-limit-only (lean: rate-limit + in-body client-auth)

decisions.rationale: Client secret is a 256-bit CSPRNG token, so bcrypt's work factor is sufficient; bcrypt@0.19.2 is STABLE, minimal-API (internal salt, hash/verify), pure-Rust (single-binary-safe). argon2 crate is mid-release-churn (0.6.0-rc; RustCrypto stable lags) and Argon2id memory-hardness is overkill for a high-entropy machine secret. KISS.
decisions.fallback: argon2 0.5 if a memory-hard KDF is later mandated
decisions.integration_notes: Verify refactors from `WHERE secret_hash=$2` lookup to fetch-client-by-id then bcrypt::verify. Format-sniff on verify ($2b$ prefix) for a clean cutover from existing SHA-256 rows. rand@0.8 already present.
open_questions: migration: format-sniff verify vs re-issue (lean: format-sniff)

choice: db transaction wrapping status-flip + audit insert (mirror db:834 .begin())
decisions.rationale: Three targeted correctness/robustness fixes on existing code, no new deps: API-key workloads authorize as Service (covered by NHI revocation); Kratos metadata_public can never promote kind; NHI revoke is audited-before-effect.

choice: verify actor_token via existing JWKS verifier OR reject present-but-unsupported (fail-closed)
wire delegate_to_hydra to proxy the exchange to the configured Hydra token endpoint (reqwest)
decisions.rationale: Silently ignoring the security-relevant actor_token param (today's behavior) is the exact fail-open pattern this phase kills; verify-or-reject closes it. Hydra proxy completes federate-first delegation (external-aud caveat ory/hydra#3723).
decisions.fallback: reject-if-present only; defer full multi-hop chaining
open_questions: verify-and-chain now vs reject-if-present this phase (lean: reject-if-present)
bcrypt migration: format-sniff verify vs re-issue
G4 actor_token verify-and-chain now vs reject-if-present
rate-limit backend: in-process governor per-endpoint vs Redis window counters (carried Redis-L2)
